Folio 8v - note in Kircher's hand.

title_name: August, Duke of Braunschweig-Lüneburg to Kircher, Wolfenbüttel, 19/29 September 1664
title_description:
August has received Kircher's "angenehmes schreiben vom letzen Augusti" and is eagerly awaiting the Mundus subterraneus: "Gott geleite das Exemplar dass es balt moege zukommen". He forwards with this letter "ein aus dem hebraischen vertirtes Buch De Templo Salomonis, die in der praefation verheissene notae, wan sie zur perfection gebracht seyn, sollen von mir alsbald ihm auch zugesandt werden". 
Cf. Kircher to Duke August, Rome, 31 October 1664, HAB: BA No.360
related_people:
Recipient: Kircher, Athanasius — Jesuit (S.J.)

title_name: Leopold Wilhelm, Archduke of Austria, to Kircher, Vienna, 3 April 1660
title_description:
Later published, with minor variants, on the third page of Kircher's Polygraphia Nova (1663). The following is a transcription of the ms letter: "Devote, syncerè Dilecte; Tametsi iam anteà per tuae Soc[ietatis] Patres complacentiam nostram singularem, quam ex transmissà tuà inventione reciproci cum omnibus populis et Nationibus instituendi Literarum Commercii cepimus, privatim significari fecimus, praetermittere tamen noluimus, quin publicis etiam his nostris Literis eam tibi testatam redderemus. Cuius enim applausum iste vastissimo ingenio tuo dignus et genuinus partus non mereatur? quo Orbis terrarum populos tot Linguarum varietate à se inuicem dissidentes, reddis quasi unilingues, et tam diversarum atque ignotarum Vocum dissonantiam ingeniosissimo communium omnibus notarum invento concordas, naturalemque humanae Linguae imbecillitatem arte mutisque Literis supplere doces. Gratulamur tibi de egregia isthac ingenii tui felicitate, teque hortamur, ut id ipsum ulteriùs excolendo, Lucubrationes tuas posteritatis memoriae consecrare, et per hoc benevolentiam Principum promereri nullatenùs desinas, è quorum numero Nos inprimis te omnigeno Archiducalis n[ost]rae gratiae adfectu complectimur; Dabantur Viennae 3.a Aprilis 1660".
Folio 50v: "Leopoldus Archidux", in Kircher's hand.
related_people:
Recipient: Kircher, Athanasius — Jesuit (S.J.)
Sender: Leopold Wilhelm, Archduke of Austria — Archduke of Austria
Signatory: Koch, Johann Georg
Signatory: Kaltschmidt, Johann

title_name: Leopoldo de' Medici to Kircher, Florence, 17 May 1667
title_description:
In order that Kircher should not lack works printed in Florence, Leopoldo sends him a work "in genere d'Anatomia" published "presentemente" by a non-Italian author, currently in Florence. The work in question is almost certainly Niels Stensen's Elementorum myologiae specimen, seu, Musculi descriptio geometrica cui accedunt canis carchariae dissectum caput, et dissectus piscis ex canum genere. - Florentiae : Ex typographia sub signo stellae, 1667. The Danish anatomist Stensen (or Steno or Stenone) was resident in Florence, in the court of Grand Duke Ferdinand II. Leopoldo also discusses the ceremonies in Florence to mark the death of the Queen mother of France.
Folio 62v: inscribed "Princeps Leopoldo" in Kircher's hand.
related_people:
Quoted Name: Stensen, Niels — anatomist, natural philosopher
Recipient: Kircher, Athanasius — Jesuit (S.J.)
Sender: Medici, Leopoldo de' — Cardinal, Prince of Tuscany
topics: printing, Florence, France, ceremonies, funerals, books, patronage, anatomy

title_name: Cesare Facchinetti to Kircher, Naples, 19 February 1667
title_description:
Facchinetti has enriched his library with an unidentified work of Kircher's on obelisks (most probably Obelisci aegyptiaci interpretatio, Rome, 1666). He praises Kircher's work over Bernini's elephant supporting the obelisk of Santa Maria sopra Minerva, pointing out that the latter displays only "maraviglia" whereas Kircher's interpretations display both "maraviglia" and "intelligenza, quella che rende più preziosi I marmi dell'antichità".
Folio 64v: address and seal

title_description: Inscription in Kircher's hand: "Hae litterae propria manu Josephi Caroli Archiducis exaratae fuerunt". The young Archduke thanks Kircher for the gift of his Mathematical Organ, sent to him by way of Fr. Pizzoni. The archduke appreciates the gift of a machine that will allow him to understand the mathematical disciplines easily, and says that the instrument is pleasing him "magis magisque".

title_name: Kircher to Ernest, Bishop of Osnabrück, Rome, 30 October 1665 (draft)
title_description:
Kircher sends a copy of Mundus Subterraneus, "opera per gli segreti ed arcani della Natura" to Ernest and his consort, as an expression of his gratitude for the kindness they showed to Kircher during their visit to Rome. The book, Kircher elaborates, was intended to give "qualche sodisfattione alla lodevole curiosità di Principi virtuosi".

title_description: "Wir zweiffeln nicht, der H. Pater werde unser vorige schreiben erhalten haben, oder theils vor ankunfft dieses noch erhalten, Und alss uns newlicher Tagen von Unserem Bergwercke eub neues gepraege zugekommen welches 5 Reichsthaler an silber in sich heilt, so haben Wir davon eines in goldt verfertigen lassen welches wir dem H. Patris // [81v] Olivae primam partem Italicarum Concionum....".

title_name: August, Duke of Braunschweig-Lüneburg to Kircher, Wolfenbüttel, 28 May 1666
title_description:
"Das Kaestlein mit den Syrischen Evangelistario und kostbaren Oelen und Sassern ist endlich vergangenen Freytag den 25. Hujus St. V. ankommen, und alles woll conditioniret gelifert. Des Herrn Generalis P. Oliva andere Theil seiner Predigten haben Wir auch woll erhalten, moechten den ersten Theil Italiaenisch auch woll haben, vertiret haben wir beyde Theile in Latein vermoege Unseres vorigen schreibens. Wir moechten woll wissen, wessen das Wapen ist, so auff beyde Buecher ausserhalb getrueckt, darinnen lauter Lewen, und unten zwey Hirschgeweihe. Die sehr woll riechende quarti und die 3 ventil gefallen Unserer Consortae sehr woll. Der Truecker zu Amsterdam wird das Iter Hetruscum vielleicht noch nicht fertig haben, sonsten thun Wir uns bedancken, wegen Ueberlassung des so raren siebenhundert Jaehrigen Syrischen Exemplars, und soll uns sonderlich lieb seyn, weiln es von dem Hern Patre hergekommen, haben es gelich unter Unsere Msta. Auff. Der Bibliotheck setzen lassen. Wir haben Unsern Johann Georg Ankeln befohlen, cito euch 400 Rthl.per wechweìsel zu uebermachen wormit ihr vor die mahl wollet vorlieb nehmen". The letter discusses August's gift of 400 Reichsthaler to Kircher, also mentioned in Kircher's Vita (1684), but placed in the year 1661.
Folio 83v: inscription (in Kircher's hand?) ""Augustus 400 scu[di]""." Fletcher, Athanasius Kircher und, p. 133.

title_name: August, Duke of Braunschweig-Lüneburg to Kircher, Wolfenbüttel, 7/17 August 1665
title_description:
Thanks K. for medicines and "chemical things" from pharmacy, but adds "that the correct dosage should be written on them, and also which sickness they are for" August has "seine drey schreiben vom 21. Febr: 8. Maii und 25 Julii woll erhalten", points out however "dass darin angedeutete Buch aber, nemblich die Historia Eustachio Marianam ist Uns nicht geliefert". [f. 84v] "Sonsten ist Uns lieb, dass ihm das geringe geldt geliefert, Er wird also darmit vor diesmahl vorlieb nehmen, und bedarff dasselbe einer so grossen dancksagung gar nichtm es ist nur zu einer geringen anzeige Unserer bestendigen zu ihm tragenden affection. Das Iter Hetruscum wird woll zulesen seyn, wan der Gran Duca la Republica di Luca auch hette, welches aber der Pabst und Der Kaiser als ihre Patroni nicht zugeben werden, koente er sich Regem Hetruria schreiben, // [f. 85r] dass der H.P. ihm gefallen lassen, obgemeltes Iter Hetruscum Uns zu dediciren, daraus verspueren Wir seine gute bestendige affection, die Wir jederzeit vermercket werden es dancknehmig zuerkennen wissen. Wirverspueren auch dieselbe noch weiter daraus, dass er Uns von Chymischen sachen aus ihrer Apotheck mittheilen wolle, es werden Uns dieselbe sehr angenehm seyn, und sehgen gerne, dass der Dosis allezeit dabey geschreiben werden moechte, und gegen was fuer Kranckheiten dieselbe nuetzlich zugebrauchen, dan wan man den Dosin nicht recht wuesste, man leichtlich an statt des nutzes schaden daran erfahren wuerde. Wie fueglich alles auf Augsspurg verschaffen, wird ihm Unser Anckel an die Handt gebenm sofern es nicht albereit von ihm geschehen. Was der Josephus Campanus vom [symbol for Saturn] und [symbol for Jupiter] herauss geben, ist woll zumercken, der Tubus hat uns viel entdecket, so den Alten unbewust gewesen, wegen mangel des scharffen gesichtes. Der Galilaeus in Florentz hat de Confectione Tuborum insonderheit seine gedancken heraus gegeben. // [f. 85v] Was der H. Pater de abditis numerorum mysteriis geschreiben, wollen wir angedeuteter massen von ihm erwarten. Es hat vor 51 Jahren Petrus Bungus Bergomas auch einer Tractat de mumerorum mysteriis ausgehen lassen, so in Unserer Bibliotheck verhanden, ist getrucket zu Bergamo Anno 1614 in 4to und lautet dessen Titul also: Petri Bungi Bergomatis Numerorum mysteria ex abditis plurimarum disciplinarum fontibus hausta. Dass wegen der Succession in die verlassene Fuerstenthuember Unsers newlich denati Agnati Christiani Ludovici, streitt zwischen den Herrn Gebruedern Georgio Wilhelmio und Johanne Friderico entstanden, wird ohnezweiffel Fama auch ueber die Alpes gebracht haben, und zwar so heftig, dass uterque militem conscriberet gehabt, es ist aber Gott sey danck derselbe ex fundamentis cum utiusque hoc contento numehr beygeleget, welche bona nova Wir dem H. Pat: auch mittheilen wollen, es heisset ja billig, Pacem te possimus Omnes. Es hat viele muehe gekostet, dieses glimmende feuer zudempffen, und die Laender und Fuerstenthuember in frieden zuerhalten".
Draft at HAB: BA No. 369/2

title_name: August, Duke of Braunschweig-Lüneburg to Kircher, Wolfenbüttel, 21/31 January 1666
title_description:
"Wir haben seine angenaeme drey schreiben, vom 1, und 22. Aug. Dell'Anno passato, und 8. Jan 1666 aus Rom, ueber Venedig, Augsburg und Nuroberg wol erhalten. Es hatte der H. Pater es nicht noetig gehabt, eine so hohe Dancksagung wegen der uebermachten quadrini, zu ueberscreiben. Er wolle nuhr mit denselben fuer dyses mahl verlyb naemen. Was er uns sonsten an Buechern, und einen Ms uebersenden wird, solches sol uns lyb und gantz angenaem kommen. Wir schikken Ihm hierbey eine Reformatione Calendarii eines Nuernbergischen professoris zu Altdorff [i.e. Abdias Trew, Gruendliche Kalender Kunst in zwei Theil verfasst, Lueneburg, 1666] und moechten wol des H. patris Judicium, wan Er so weit a magis feriis abmuessigen koente, darueber vernaemen wy man astronomice ex politice der lang begehrten, und vielmaal tartirten, wiewol // [f.86v] bishero undurno Enderung schaffen moechte".
Folio 87v: inscription (in Kircher's hand?): "Augustus dux gratias agit de transmissis medicamentis et extenuat munus subsidiarium transmissum".
Draft at HAB: BA No. 369/1

title_name: August, Duke of Braunschweig-Lüneburg to Kircher, Wolfenbüttel, 16/26 April 1666
title_description:
August wishes to obtain G.P. Oliva's books for his library. 
Folio 88r: He has received Kircher's letter of 19 March and 2 April "worinnen auch zugleich des Herrn P. Generalis Johan: Pauli Olivae S.J. Tomus 2. Concionum Apostolicarum italico idiomate conscriptarum geleget. Die lateinische version Tomi prioris et posterioris desselben Concionum, selche der Johan: de Bussiers quisdem S. in lateinischer sprache heraus gegeben, haben wir in Unserer Bibliothek. Primum librum earundem concionum in Italianischer // [f. 88v] sprache moechten Wir umb die gebuerm zu completierung derselben, auch woll haben, umb Uns damit in der Italiaenischen sprache zuueben, so wir in annis 89. 90 und 600 in Jubliaeo, tempore P. M. Clementis 8vi. P.m. in etwas gefasset".

Folio 98v: inscription (in Kircher's hand): "Litterae ad Joseph. Carl. Archiduc."

title_name: Kircher to Alexander VII, Pope (Fabio Chigi), no place, undated (draft)
title_description:
Kircher asks the Pope for permission to be granted for Elizabeth of Palatine, wife of the Duke of Luneburg, to be granted permission to visit the Collegio Romano, including its library and Kircher's Museum as well as the Domus Professa, arguing that although the princess is not of the Catholic she is "ab ea tamen non aliena", and "virorum literatorum praesertim Soc. nostrae, quibuscum iam dudum in Germania assueverat, commercium ambit". Kircher notes that the power to grant such permission is reserved to the Pope alone: "respondi eiusmodi facultatem neque me, neque P. Generalem, neque quenquam alium concedete posse, utpote soli Summo Pontifici, cui iure religionis omnes subdimur, reservatam".
Folio 102v: in Kircher's hand "Ad Alex. VII P.M. pro Palatina".
Draft

title_name: Kircher to August, Duke of Braunschweig-Lüneburg, no place, undated [Rome, 19 March 1666] (draft)
title_description:
Kircher forwards to August a manuscript of the four gospels in Estranghelo-Syriac, written in the form of Syriac used in Palestine at the time of Christ.
Folio 114v: inscribed in Kircher's hand "Serm. Augusto Duc. Brun." Kircher also forwards to August the work of G.P. Oliva (General of the Society of Jesus) Conciones Apostolicarum, and "olea pretiosa et aquae odoriferae separatis arculis inclusae: servientque tum ad spiritus contentione studiorum exhaustos instaurandos".
The copy of the letter actually sent to August (dated 19 March 1666) is in HAB 6 Novissimi 2o ff. 59-61
Draft

title_name: Johann Friedrich, Duke of Braunschweig-Lüneburg to Kircher, Herzberg, 14 April 1664
title_description:
Thanks Kircher for a gift of the Polygraphia Nova, forwarded by way of Duke August, Johann Friedrich's cousin. Describes the book as "… un libro Intitolato Poligrafia p[er] aprendere le lingue, à mè diretto dalla P. V. Aut.re del med.mo Veram.te il Nome Kirchero essaltato e lodato dà più d'una lingua, era dovere, che finalm.te comparisse ancora in forma di lingue p[er] informare con artificiosa Poligrafia la Nobilità Peregrina, et I Prencipi Curiosi".
Folio 117v: address and seal, with (in a different hand) "Don Federico, Duca di Brunswick."

title_name: Leopoldo de' Medici to Kircher, Florence, 15 May 1666
title_description:
Leopoldo sends Kircher a copy of Giovanni Alfonso Borelli's work Theoricae Mediceorum planetarum ex causis physicis deductae à Io. Alphonso Borellio. - Florentiae :ex Typographia S.M.D., 1666. "L'intendimento cosi perfetto che V. P. possiede di tante scienze et il diletto che ella ritrova in rimirare l'altruj virtuose fatiche mi persuadono giustamente adesso ad inviarlene alcune date presentamente alla luce dal Nostro Matematico Dottor Borelli sopra le Cause fisiche de moti, et apparenze dei Pianetini di Giove. Et parendomi di riconoscere in questa Operetta speculazioni cosi curiose, et stimabili, non ho dubbio che la Paternità Vostra sia per cortesemente gradirla".
Folio 127v: "Leopoldo Principe di Toscana" (in Kircher's hand?)

title_name: Christian August, Duke of Schleswig-Holstein, to Kircher, Osterholm, 27 November 1661
title_description:
Christian August (not Christiaan Huygens, as earlier inventories of the correspondence suggest), who had visited Kircher in Rome earlier in the year, describes the island, and provides Kircher with news about German dignitaries. "Questa Isola nutrisce 2 compagnie delle truppe regie, et è fatigata giornalmente per le continue contributioni. Se dice ch'il Conte Ulfeld sarà liberato dalla sua prigione, la cio certezza sapremo subito. M'ora proposto d'andar alla corte, ma aspettaro fin ch'habbia risposta sopra alcune lettere // [151v] ivi mandate. E mentre non ho niente da fare passo il mio tempo con ricogliere nella memoria e perleggere i suoi libri". Thanks Kircher for his "fatighe et amabili instruttioni", and adds that "Il Sig. Holsten si bacia le mani. Riverisco Il P[ad]re Montesio"". 
Folio 152v: address and seal
related_people:
Recipient: Kircher, Athanasius — Jesuit (S.J.)
Sender: Christian August, Duke of Schleswig-Holstein — Duke of Schleswig-Holstein

title_description:
Thanks Kircher for letter, and gift of "il suo pregiato volume", possibly the Polygraphia Nova. The Marchese adds that "con grand[issi]mo gusto mi son trattenuto molto tempo nel legger li suoi scritti publicati al mondo della luce de magnetismi della musica et della sapienza egittiana". He impatiently awaits the Mundus Subterraneus".

title_name: Juan Caramuel Lobkowic to Kircher, Naples, 24 March 1665
title_description:
Discussion, with diagram, of the issue of whether the comet of 1664 was above or below the sun, based on considerations of parallax. Caramuel, who judges that the comet was below the sun, has received Kircher's work on the comet (Gioseffo Petrucci [Athanasius Kircher], Fisiologia Nuova della Natura delle Comete, Rome, 1665). He has also received a table of the motion of the comet according to Meyer.
Folio 157v: "D. Caramuel de Concu." in different hand

Sender: Caramuel Lobkowic, Juan — Cistercian (O. Cist.), Bishop of Kempten, Benedictine (O.S.B.), abbot

title_name: Kircher to Johann II Kasimir, King of Poland, Rome, 20 January 1664 (draft)
title_description:
Folio 179r: [pencil note] "Regi Poloniae gli offre la sua chiave per intendere e scrivere in qualunque lingua. De qua vede". [Polygraphia Nova] "…la supplicherà a nome mio di accettare come piccolo e povero tributo d'un grand obsequio, un mio libro uscito ultimamente alle stampe, per intendere e scrivere in qualunque lingua, ancor que non si n'abbi cognitione; insieme con una cifra per aprire il proprio segreto, a chi si vuole, con renderlo impenetrabile a qualunque altro. Due simili ne furono presentate ultimamente, una alla Santità di N.S. L'altra alla Maestà Cesarea, e doppo essere state messe alla prova [179v] riportorono la loro approvatione…"
Folio 180v: "Regem Poloniae"
Draft

title_name: Francesco Manuele Melos to Kircher, Genoa, 10 January 1665
title_description:
"Rmo Padre: mio caro amico e stimatissimo Maestro. Questo Cometa a qualche persona, sarà infausto mà a la mia à stato faustissimo; essendo ocassione di havere io recivuto la cortessissima lettera de la molto R. P. V…" Manuel has been asked by Kircher to send him observations of a comet. He also offers to send "semplici, e cosse rare", and also get hold of "osservatione fatte dela setentrionale, come de gli Inglesse e Holandesse…" 
Folio 182v: "Don Frances. Manuel de Melos"

title_name: Carlo Calà, Duke of Diano, to Kircher, Naples, 21 March 1665
title_description:
"Ricevo il favore di V.P., con il libretto inviatomi sopra l'osservatione della Cometa, quale hò letto subito con particolara mio gusto, come opera d'un tanto Maestro, et mio singolar padrone et amico, anzi l'hò prometto à molti Cavalieri che l'anderanno leggendo da mano in mano. Io non posso darli giudizio sopra cosa che non è della mia professione, et così solam.te hò pasciuto la curiosità, et imparato qualche cosa, ma posso bene assecurarli che non è senza prodigio, nè sarà vacua d'effetti maravigliosi, et così lo farò vedere a V.P. molto presto; intanto resto baciando à V.P. infinitam.te le mani".
Folio 206v: "Il Duca di Diano" in different hand.
related_people:
Recipient: Kircher, Athanasius — Jesuit (S.J.)
Sender: Calà, Carlo — Duke of Diano
topics: comets, astronomical observations, reading, Fisiologia Nuova della Natura delle Comete, gifts, books, astronomy

title_name: Ferdinando Giovanni di Gonzaga to Ulrich Gering, Castiglione, 24 August 1663
title_description: Not originally to Kircher, but Gheringo. The second paragraph starts: "Dal Pre Athanasio hò ricevuto la nuova opera sopra l'intelligenza di tutti i linguaggi del Mondo, la faccio ligare, e veram.te è cosa curios.ma, bell.ma, e degna d'un tanto qualificato Padre. Premiti al med.o la mia Medaglia d'oro, e gli hò ratificata la promessa anche in questa congiuntura, ne hò tardato ad effet: tuarla, se non perche desiderano, che fosse ben'improntata, e colla magg.re delicatezza, ma non havendo trovato artefice di mia sodisfatt.e sin hora, ne hò havuta m.ta passione…"

Padre, colla mia effigie, improntata in Medaglia, mà d'intiera sua, e mia sodisfattione, e perfettam.te bene, ch'auguro à lei compito, per fine.

title_name: Prince of Holstein (Christian August, Duke of Schleswig-Holstein?) to Guglielmo Montesio, Paris, 6 July 1661
title_description:
Folio 267r: Montesio passed on the "segreto delli archibuzzi à vento" "… il n[ost]ro studio mathematico non avanza quì [in Paris] cosi bene come nella gratissima galleria del R.P. Kircher"
Folio 267v: A.G. is making a "Polemoscopio molto utile nella guerra per scoprirne con securità l'Inimico quando sta vicino nella fossa ove altrove." + diagram of magnifying periscope, which he did not remember seeing in Rome. Also describes a "camera oscura portatile", with a diagram, which rights the image.
Folio 268r: "Non ci manca altro che di poter comporre melothesie [?] convenienti p[er] le commedie" (this is described as one of Kircher's secrets). He found a single copy of the Arte Lucis et Umbrae in Paris, costing 10 scudi. In Rome, he bought copies of the Arte Magnetica and the Musurgia for "due dobble". Afterwards, continuing their journey, "via nostro paese", to England.
Folio 268v: address and seal, "Princeps Holsatia" in a different hand.

title_name: Apollonius of Perga, Conics, beginning of Book V (in Arabic)
title_description:
Part of the Title 2901.
Fasciculus Epistolarum Latina, Gallica, Graeca, Hebraica, Chaldaica, Syriaca Arabica, Aethiopica, Samaritana alijsque peregrinis linguis ad me a peritissimij harum linguis antiquitatumque variarum conscriptarum in quibus maxime respondetur adea quae de antiquitatibus Aegyptiacis ijs ab Authore huisque operis fuerunt proposita ijsdem linguis.

This text has been identified by Jan Hogendijk, of the University of Utrecht, who suggests that it is the revision of al-Isfahani or al-Shirazi brought to Europe from Constantinople by Christianus Ravius and exhibited to various scholars in Holland between 1642 and 1647. Only books I to IV of the Conics survived in Greek, and were first translated in an edition by Giovanni-Battista Memmo at Venice in 1537. This edition, which contained many errors, was replaced by Federico Commandino's translation, published in Bologna in 1566. Various versions of books V-VII in Arabic circulated in Europe during the seventeenth century. In 1629, Jacobus Golius brought a Codex of the Conics to Leiden, and planned to publish a translation of the lost books. In the 1640s, the English mathematician John Pell worked on a translation of another manuscript of the Conics, brought back from Constantinople by Christian Ravius. Information from Apollonius Conics Books V to VII: The Arabic Translation of the Lost Greek Original in the Version of the Banu Musa, edited with translation and commentary by G.J. Toomer, New York: Springer-Verlag, 1990, vol. 1, xx-xxv, and Fuat Sezgin, Geschichte des Arabischen Schrifttums, Leiden: Brill, 1974, Band V, p. 141.
related_people: Author: Apollonius, of Perga
